channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built an image compressor that hits an exact KB size — and never uploads your files
item.description: TL;DR: Online forms demand images under a hard limit — "signature under 20 KB," "photo under 50 KB." Most compressors make you upload your file to a server to hit that. I built SwiftShrink to do...

item.title: 🧩 Your MapStruct Mappers Are Hiding Null Bugs
item.description: Someone adds a field to a DTO. The entity already has it. MapStruct compiles fine, the tests pass, the PR merges. Two weeks later the field is null in production and nobody knows why. The compiler...

item.title: Event Loop - Entendendo uma das bases do Node
item.description: O Event Loop é o mecanismo responsável por decidir quando callbacks e continuidades de operações assíncronas devem ser executados. Ele não executa operações de I/O diretamente, mas organiza a...

item.title: Storied Colors – a catalogue of named colors
item.description: Article URL: https://storiedcolors.com/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48577374 Points: 6 # Comments: 0

item.title: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are ‘unsustainable’ and Apple is going to raise prices
item.description: Apple is planning to raise prices in response to the ongoing memory shortage. In an interview with The Wall Street Journal, Apple CEO Tim Cook says "price increases are unavoidable:" We're doing...

item.title: Stop Picking Dashboard Icons by Keyword
item.description: Most dashboard icon problems do not come from bad icons. They come from good icons used with the wrong meaning. You search for users, pick a clean SVG icon, place it in the sidebar, and move on....

item.title: Using LLM for Dialogue Management
item.description: Dialogue management is the process of tracking conversational state and deciding what an agent should say or do next. Classical systems split this into isolated modules: natural language...

item.title: WIP - Glossário DevOps #1
item.description: Texto com base no livro "Manual de DevOps" WIP significa "Work In Progress". É uma métrica essencial que representa a quantidade de trabalho iniciado, mas ainda não concluído. Na prática, ela...

item.title: FIFA Hack Authentication Flaw, Chrome Ad Blocker End, AI Supply Chain Security
item.description: FIFA Hack Authentication Flaw, Chrome Ad Blocker End, AI Supply Chain Security Today's Highlights Today's top security news covers a critical real-world authentication vulnerability, significant...

item.title: LLM Fallback in Production, Agentic eCommerce, and GitHub Copilot for Parallel Agents
item.description: LLM Fallback in Production, Agentic eCommerce, and GitHub Copilot for Parallel Agents Today's Highlights This week highlights practical applications and architectural considerations for AI...

item.title: Why Can't Walnut Creek Build 3 Bedroom Apartments with a Playground?
item.description: Article URL: https://kevin.burke.dev/kevin/walnut-creek-single-stair/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48577246 Points: 4 # Comments: 1

item.title: VSCO launches Studio Pro mobile photo editing app and plans $500 per year subscription
item.description: VSCO is taking on Adobe with a new Studio Pro editing app rolling out today on iOS and coming to macOS later this year, as Bloomberg reports. At launch, the app offers tools for batch editing,...

item.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
item.description: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

item.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
item.description: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through...

item.title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
item.description: The affected include Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, a NATO contractor, and Fortinet.

item.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
item.description: Tesco claimed Broadcom hiked its VMware prices by about 175 percent in UK court filings.

item.title: Epic wants to let you bring your Fortnite skins to other games
item.description: Epic Games has been touting the potential of an interoperable metaverse for years, though that vision hasn't yet become a reality. But with Unreal Engine 6, the next major version of its game...

item.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
item.description: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

item.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
item.description: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

item.title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
item.description: Anthropic has spent much of this week fighting to get its newest AI models back online after the Trump administration abruptly ordered the company to cut access for all foreign nationals,...

item.title: Operating a Humanoid With Your Body Is a Hot Job in China’s Hardware Capital
item.description: In Shenzhen, workers at IO-AI Tech control humanoid robots using a VR rig reminiscent of Ready Player One.

item.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
item.description: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

item.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research. The...

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck on AI IPOs, personal agents, and the ROI reckoning
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through...

item.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
item.description: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.

item.title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
item.description: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

item.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
item.description: Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet. The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier...

item.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
item.description: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots...

item.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
item.description: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.
